item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I resized it. Thanks for the feedback. The dimensions are now 806 x 821 pixels and the image is now a .jpg file.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: General note, author, I can assist with resizing images.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent series of tags were removed, as there were a few tags missing.

Readers, if you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just here to say that you have one image that takes up 4.21 MB, which took about half an hour to load. I'd recommend compressing that image and then putting back up here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just fixed most of these. Thanks for the suggestions!

item.title: Re: Image scources.
item.content:encoded: I replaced the images. The third is now a photo of my actual house, but I haven't found a CC Compliant image for SCP-4230-B yet. If people like it better without the second image, I'll keep it like this. However, I'll look for a replacement in the meantime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's just so much of this that I don't like that I don't think I can upvote it.

Here's some problems.
– hide block

Keter

This is a stationary anomaly with no really threat of breaching containment. Make it Euclid if you want, I would argue that it is Safe though.

Sites █,█, ██, ██, and ██ are to enter lockdown mode immediately

Don't redact the containment procedures. How are we supposed to know what to lock down?

SCP-4230 is a crudely constructed snowman

This is something I debated about a lot to myself, is "snowman" clinical? I'm going to go with no for now, as it feels like a nickname to me, similar to "hopscotch" or "Four-Square". I would probably place a concise description of what a snowman is or something and then "colloquially referred to as a "snowman"" at the end possibly. This is just my personal opinion, and is up for debate.

When human and animal subjects

"animal subjects" feels like it's trying to be clinical, I feel like "vertebrates" or "organisms" would work better as a whole.

SCP-4230-1 is an anomalous disease

I don't get why this is a disease. It could just be a thing that occurs to everything in the range that's living, I just don't feel like it has to be a disease or a whole new sub-designation.

Instances of SCP-4230-A, while not inherently anomalous in nature

huh? Saying that they're not really anomalous right after explaining how they come from transmuted people and right before you say that the produce eggs makes no sense.

SCP-4230-B instances are extremely hostile lifeforms produced by instances of SCP-4230-A.

This feels redundant as you just explained to us that they come from -A's.

instances have been shown to reach adulthood in the span of 15 seconds.

How are you classifying "adulthood" of snowmen?

and will retreat if large amounts are present

"Large amounts" isn't a descriptor I would use here, Maybe just replace the sentence with:

"Instances of SCP-4230-B dislike high temperature areas and will actively evade said areas."

Or something similar.

Attempting to retaliate against an SCP-4230-B attack with snow or ice will result in [DATA EXPUNGED].

If you're just going to expunge point of the sentence then you might as well delete the sentence as a whole.

Test Log 4230-B-1

This log as a whole doesn't really add much, and the last test just feels to unprofessional for me. I would ask yourself how much would be lost if you got rid of it in its entirety.

Name redacted per request.

What? This makes no sense, it makes it seem like this is some sort of news outlet instead of a government organization. Why should the Foundation care if you don't want your name shown in a top secret government document. To add onto that, you don't need to blackbox all of the proper nouns, it gets irritating after there's too many black boxes in one place.

Incident Report 4230-1

This report in its entirety really doesn't add much to the narrative of the SCP at all and wouldn't affect anything if it was just deleted.

Overall, it's got some good bones, but I just feels so rough and like a Series I article for all of the wrong reasons (the "funny test/addendum" stuff, the sheer amount of blackboxing and expunging). If the clinical tone were better and you were to make it more smoothed out in some aspects then I possibly could upvote.

item.title: Re: Image scources.
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I did some more detective work, I'm pretty sure they aren't CC Compliant. I don't even like the 2nd image that much tbh, it doesn't match up that well with the description and overall just feels… fake to me. Like yeah it's fake, but it doesn't look real at all.

item.title: Re: Image scources.
item.content:encoded: I really doubt the last two images used are CC compliant, and honestly I think the first image works enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I must say, this is a creative

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this is a single, immobile object that only affects things that get close to it, why is it Keter? Surely Euclid would fit better?

item.title: Image scources.
item.content:encoded:
So reverse image search results.

Image 1: couldn't find anything, probably made by the author
Image 2: Oldest I could find was a post on 9gag (link)
Image 3: Oldest I could find was from a wallpaper site (link)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see you like reading Calvin and Hobbes as well.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, all. I'm proud to announce my second SCP! I tried to focus more on a body horror theme, unlike my last one, SCP-4072. I also tried to make it a lot less long-winded.

Nevertheless, thanks for reading.

Credits

SCP-4230 Photograph and the picture of the house were taken by me
